reasoning: France are the clear market and model favorite (around +150), already booked into the semifinals as the only side to win all five matches in normal time while outscoring opponents 16-2 behind Mbappé, Dembélé and Olise. Their sternest remaining threat is a semifinal against Spain, who are unbeaten in 36 games and boast a record World Cup clean-sheet streak, so that tie effectively functions as an early final on the tougher half of the draw. The opposite bracket is weaker and more open: England edged Mexico at altitude and rely on Kane and Bellingham, while defending champions Argentina have looked ragged, needing dramatic comebacks against Cape Verde and Egypt with an aging squad. Norway are the live outsider — Haaland is in devastating form after knocking out Brazil, and they sit on the softer side of the bracket with all the pressure on their opponents.
